content: Hostel curfew is strictly enforced. Girls must return to hostel by 8:30 PM. Boys must enter the main gate by 9:00 PM and hostel by 9:30 PM. Late entry is not allowed without prior permission from the warden.

Hostel attendance is mandatory every night between 9:00 PM and 10:15 PM through biometric systems. In some hostels, attendance may extend until 10:30 PM. Missing attendance leads to penalties and possible parent notification.
Biometric systems are used to track student movement and attendance. Authorities can check whether a student is inside or outside the campus at any time.
Students are free to move inside campus during daytime. Leaving campus during the day is allowed without formal permission before evening curfew.
On working days, students are allowed outing once per day during non-class hours for approximately 2 hours. They must return before 7:00 PM at the main gate and 8:30 PM at the hostel.
On weekends and institutional holidays, students are allowed outing once per day for approximately 4 hours, subject to curfew timings.
Overnight stay outside campus requires prior permission from the warden and parental consent. Students must submit expected return date.
First-year students are not allowed to leave campus for the first 2 to 3 months after admission and are subject to stricter monitoring.
Girls hostels have stricter rules including earlier curfew at 8:30 PM, restricted outing, mandatory parental consent for leaves, and stricter monitoring by wardens.
Boys are allowed to leave hostel from 5:30 AM for gym or sports activities. Girls are allowed to leave only after 6:00 AM.
Students staying late for academic work, lab, club activities, or library must obtain a night pass or night slip approved by faculty or warden.
The library is open until 12:00 AM. Students returning late must carry a valid night slip to re-enter hostel.
Silence hours are enforced from 9:00 PM to 6:00 AM. Students must avoid noise, music, and gatherings during this time.
Birthday celebrations and parties inside hostel rooms or corridors are not allowed, especially during silence hours.
Students must stay only in their allotted rooms. Staying in other rooms or unauthorized shifting is not allowed.
Visitors and non-VITians are not allowed inside hostel premises. Opposite gender interaction is allowed only in designated visiting areas.
Students can give a maximum of 25 clothes per laundry cycle. Each student is allowed a total of 44 laundry washes per academic year.
Hostel mess timings are fixed. Breakfast is from 7:00 AM to 9:00 AM, Lunch is from 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM, Snacks are from 4:30 PM to 6:15 PM, and Dinner is from 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM.
Breakfast is served from 7:00 AM to 9:00 AM.
Lunch is served from 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM.
Snacks are served from 4:30 PM to 6:15 PM.
Dinner is served from 7:00 PM to 9:00 PM.
In case of any emergency, students must immediately contact the hostel warden or assistant warden.
Alcohol consumption, possession, or entering campus in a drunken state is strictly prohibited and may lead to suspension.
Use, possession, or distribution of drugs or narcotics leads to immediate expulsion.
Smoking inside hostel premises is strictly prohibited and attracts penalties.
Ragging or harassment of any kind leads to immediate expulsion.
Fighting or causing injury leads to fines, suspension, or expulsion depending on severity.
Cybercrime or defaming the institute leads to expulsion.
Theft or stealing results in suspension from the institute.
Electrical appliances such as heaters, kettles, induction stoves, and irons are not allowed in hostel rooms.
Cooking inside hostel rooms is strictly prohibited.
Motor vehicles are not allowed inside hostel premises. Only bicycles are permitted.
Students must carry their ID card at all times. Entry without ID leads to penalties.
Late entry without permission leads to fines starting from Rs.100 and increasing with repeated violations.
Missing hostel attendance may result in penalties, suspension of outing privileges, and parent notification.
Repeated violation of outing rules leads to suspension of outing privileges and disciplinary action.
Absent without leave leads to heavy penalties and possible suspension.
Damaging hostel or institute property leads to fines and compensation charges.
Serious violations can lead to suspension, expulsion, and loss of privileges such as placements or scholarships.
Students are not allowed to stay in hostel during vacations unless they have academic work.
Hostels are meant for academic focus, discipline, and maintaining a proper study environment.
